structural_claim: Quantum systems exhibit collective modes that emerge from many-body interactions, creating coherent oscillations that represent new degrees of freedom beyond single-particle descriptions

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Nuclei exhibit collective oscillation modes where protons and neutrons oscillate coherently, demonstrating quantum fluids with emergent collective degrees of freedom
Nonlinear optical media support stable collective excitations where many photons form coherent, shape-preserving structures through nonlinear interactions
